page: Google Cloud Platform a4x-highgpu-4g is an accelerator-optimized virtual machine belonging to the a4x server family. It features 140 dedicated vCPUs based on the x86_64 CPU architecture, paired with 884.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 6.31 GB of RAM per core.

The primary hardware feature of this server is the inclusion of four bundled NVIDIA GB200 GPUs. This configuration does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met through network-attached options. There are zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this instance.

From a resource density perspective, the combination of high-count dedicated vCPUs and multiple enterprise GPUs positions this server for heavy parallel processing. The hardware profile is designed for workloads such as deep learning training, large language model inference, and high-performance computing tasks that require massive GPU acceleration and substantial system memory.
description: Google Cloud Platform a4x-highgpu-4g is an accelerator-optimized virtual machine designed for intensive parallel computing. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 140 dedicated vCPUs and 884.0 GB of system memory, providing 6.31 GB of RAM per core. The server is equipped with four bundled NVIDIA GB200 GPUs to accelerate compute-heavy workloads. Local storage is not included, requiring external storage solutions, and there are no complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This hardware profile is optimized for workloads such as large-scale machine learning, deep learning training, and complex data science simulations requiring high-density GPU resources.
